notes.anchor: you let me know so quickly about Caesar's letter
notes.note: After Pompey's defeat at Pharsalus, Cicero waited anxiously at Brundisium in 47 BC for word of his standing with the victorious Caesar. News of a letter from Caesar bore directly on whether Cicero would be pardoned.

notes.anchor: the fourth day before the Nones of June
notes.note: The Nones fell on the 5th in June, so by inclusive Roman reckoning this is 2 June (47 BC). Cicero opens with the formulaic health-greeting (si vales, bene est), a convention he usually drops in intimate letters.
